member.office_address: 1026 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4902

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Pocan FY27 Appropriations Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Please submit your FY27 programmatic and language requests using this form. Please note this is NOT the correct form for Community Project Funding (CPF) applications. Please contact our office at 202-225-2906 for questions about CPF submissions.

The deadline for programmatic and language requests is COB Wednesday, March 4. The form will remain available after the deadline, but please note late requests may not be considered.

Instructions: 
Please fill out one form per request.  
If you submit more than one request, please rank the importance of each request in the final question of this form. 
If you are submitting bill or report language that goes with a programmatic request, it must be submitted as a separate language request, and not included in the program description. 
To the extent possible, please provide detailed information regarding the location of your request in the subcommittee bill or report, down to the activity, account, or country level (if applicable).

sections.description: Details regarding the organization and point of contact making the request.

fields.label: Is your organization located in WI-02?

sections.description: Information on the subcommittee and type of appropriations request being made.

fields.label: Subcommittee
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.help_text: Select programmatic request, report language, or bill language.
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for an existing NASA program (Science Mission Directorate), which is a programmatic funding request.

sections.description: Specific program and agency information including funding history and requested amounts.

fields.label: Department & Agency (Ex. HHS/Administration for Children & Families)
fields.rationale: This is the top-level agency for the request.

fields.label: Bureau and/or Office (Ex. Office of Early Childhood Development/Office of Head Start)
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate (SMD) is the primary bureau for NASA's science programs and account.

fields.label: Program Title (Ex. Head Start) (Include PE # for DoD Requests)
fields.rationale: This is the specific program/account name requested in the advocacy context.

fields.label: For Programmatic Requests: Funding Level Requested for FY27
fields.help_text: Amount requested for the upcoming fiscal year.
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask amount provided in the reference values for FY27.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Funding Level
fields.rationale: This value is provided as the FY2026 enacted total in the reference materials.

fields.label: FY25 Enacted Level in CR for DHS
fields.help_text: Enacted funding level from two fiscal years prior.
fields.rationale: This field is specifically for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), but the request pertains to NASA. This section does not apply.

fields.label: Description of Programmatic Request
fields.draft_value:
* Request $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to ensure mission stability and workforce certainty.
* Sustains U.S. leadership in space exploration and strategic competition, particularly against China's rapid deep-space expansion.
* Executes National Academies' Decadal Survey priorities, advancing peer-reviewed consensus on climate monitoring and planetary discovery.
* Stimulates domestic innovation through high-skill STEM challenges, yielding an estimated $3 economic return for every $1 invested.
fields.rationale: Request value and justification based on Advocacy Talking Points and the specified $9B ask amount.

fields.label: For Language Requests: Exact Language
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request; the provided context indicates that report and bill language are not requested (N/A).

sections.description: Connection to WI-02 and submission ranking.

fields.label: Does this request have a direct WI-02 connection?
fields.rationale: WI-02 has significant NASA Science spending, including over $47 million across FY2023-FY2025. Key local recipients include the University of Wisconsin System in Madison ($24.8M in grants and $20.9M in contracts) and Bruker AXS LLC in Fitchburg.

fields.label: Description of WI-02 connection (please be as specific as possible)
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science invested $47,073,530.62 in WI-02 institutions between FY2023 and FY2025, supporting a robust local STEM workforce.
* University of Wisconsin System institutions (WI-02) have received over $45.8 million in combined award totals for critical research, including climate monitoring ($2.6M) and habitability searches ($5M).
* Fitchburg-based Bruker Axs LLC provides advanced instrumentation and materials analysis support essential for NASA space missions.
* District-led research develops advanced infrared sensors and processes data for Earth's weather, climate, and wildfire monitoring systems.
fields.rationale: Directly cites spending totals, specific recipients (UW System, Bruker Axs), and research goals (climate, wildfires, habitability) from the WI-2 NASA spending context.

fields.label: Priority Ranking (If you submitted more than one request, please indicate where this request ranks numerically across all subcommittees. If you only submitted one, just put 1)
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is a user-specific determination based on their full slate of requests.
